title: Client-side print bundle / PDF export of the full kit (no server)
context: Most field use of repair-cafe-kits is on paper in venues with poor connectivity. A downloadable, client-side print bundle or PDF export of the full kit (all checklists + organizer playbook) is the primary distribution mechanism and must work with no server. The print/PDF approach is selected by ADR #3 in arch-001. The bundle must include a visible version number and date so stale paper copies are identifiable in the field. All offline fonts must be bundled — no runtime web-font fetch.
objective: Implement a client-side print bundle or PDF export of the full kit (organizer playbook + all triage checklists) that works without a server, includes a visible version and date, bundles all fonts offline, and meets print legibility requirements.
acceptanceCriteria: A user can download or generate a print bundle / PDF of the full kit (organizer playbook + all triage checklists) from the browser with no server-side processing
The approach used matches the print/PDF ADR selected in arch-001
The bundle includes a visible version number and date on each page so stale paper copies can be identified
All fonts are bundled offline — no runtime web-font fetch; the bundle is fully usable with network disabled
Printed output meets print legibility: WCAG AA contrast, minimum font size, no color-only meaning; a print-smoke CI test confirms this
The feature introduces no telemetry, no PII capture, and no third-party scripts; pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint pass
output: A merged PR adding client-side print bundle / PDF export of the full kit with visible version + date, bundled offline fonts, and a passing print-smoke CI test confirming legibility.
